metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
Many sequential ungated tool calls in ONE turn (--trust-all-tools) stay one contiguous working span; settles ready only at the text-only AssistantMessage. JSONL carries no model/token/cost, so observations assert agent identity only.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario asserts a high-volume single-turn burst: within a turn state stays continuously `working` (no flicker to `ready` or `waiting` between successive tool-call/result pairs or between assistant text fragments), the turn settles cleanly to `ready`, and the session is never stuck. Topology `ready -> working -> ready -> working -> ready -> working -> ready -> working -> ready`, final state `ready`, at least one tool call.

**agent=yes.** `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` runs every tool autonomously with NO per-tool permission picker (the `--trust-all-tools` flag assumes persisted trust-all consent), so the agent never stops to ask the user mid-burst. A single self-contained prompt that tells kiro-cli to create files and then run many separate shell commands on them (pwd, three file creates, ls, three wc -l, cat, head, then summarize) deterministically produces a chain of many `AssistantMessage(toolUse)` -> `ToolResults` pairs in ONE turn before the final text-only `AssistantMessage`. This is the same native many-tool-call shape the committed codex cell exercises (codex has no permission gate either); unlike claudecode — whose modern Sonnet default refuses auto-mode for paid models and stalls tool-call turns at the permission gate, forcing the claudecode recipe into a 4-plain-prompt proxy — kiro-cli with --trust-all-tools captures the REAL burst. The live 2.5.1 smoke confirmed the turn shape directly: `Prompt -> AM(text+toolUse) -> ToolResults -> AM(text+toolUse) -> ToolResults -> AM(text-only)`, with the final text-only AssistantMessage being the turn-done marker (FINDINGS.md §5).

**daemon=full.** The kiro-cli parser (parser.go) maps an `AssistantMessage` carrying any `toolUse` block to `assistant` + ToolUses (EventType `assistant`, keeps the session `working`), a `ToolResults` line to `tool_result` (closing the open call), and ONLY a text-only `AssistantMessage` to `turn_done` (the single `ready` settle). A `Prompt` maps to `user_message` (drives `working`). There is no permission/`waiting` path on the trust-all launch — kiro-cli emits no AskUser/permission event into the JSONL, and the parser produces no `waiting` for any of these kinds — so an intermediate tool result is never treated as a settle or a gate, and the whole many-call burst is one contiguous `working` span. Live append survives long turns and the pending `toolUse` lands BEFORE approval (moot under --trust-all-tools), and the PID stays bound via `ExactName{kiro-cli}` + `DiscoverPIDByCWD` across the turn (FINDINGS.md §1, §2). Net observable: proc-PID pre-session born `ready`, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born `ready`, ONE `ready -> working` at the prompt, ONE contiguous `working` span across the burst (no flicker to `ready`/`waiting`), ONE `working -> ready` at the text-only AssistantMessage, final `ready`. This matches the codex cell's `daemon=full` for the structurally identical burst.

**driver=ready.** The recipe needs only `send`, `wait_turn`, and `sleep`, all three in the driver's `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` — no step type must be ported. `wait_turn` counts text-only AssistantMessages (mirroring the parser's turn_done rule) and uses a generous timeout for the deliberately long turn; the trailing sleep never races a still-running turn.

## Caveats

The spec asserts user-observable state/topology only — never the exact number of commands kiro-cli chooses to run, tool output text, or token totals (model/OS-dependent, not stable across re-records). The burst_working dwell with no-ready/no-waiting invariants is the load-bearing no-flicker assertion.

## Conclusion

Applicable: yes. kiro-cli with --trust-all-tools natively runs a long single-turn tool-call burst with no permission gate, and the daemon observes the full arc as one contiguous working span (each ToolResults closes its open call; the text-only AssistantMessage is the only ready settle). daemon=full is PROVISIONAL on emission until the live record run promotes it from a doc/code read to a captured events.jsonl.
caveats: Assert user-observable state/topology only — do NOT assert specific shell tool output text, the exact number of commands kiro-cli chooses to run, or token totals; all are model/OS-dependent and not stable across re-records.

kiro-cli's JSONL carries NO model/token/cost fields (those live only in the <uuid>.json metadata sidecar, which the v1 adapter does not read), so observations assert agent identity only — model is unknown and cost/tokens are NOT asserted nonzero (they would be empty/zero and fail). A sidecar MetricsReader is a documented future enhancement (FINDINGS.md §4).

daemon=full is PROVISIONAL on emission until the live record run: it rests on a doc/code read of the parser's no-flicker rules, not a captured many-call events.jsonl. record promotes it from provisional to settled.

The recipe keeps kiro-cli's DEFAULT model (no --model pin) to match the codex/claudecode stress recipes, which pin no model. The driver's fixed launch line is `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` with no model-injection seam; if a cheaper model is wanted later (e.g. qwen3-coder-next at 0.05x credits), it needs a driver launch-flag change, not a recipe edit. Distinct from kiro-cli x self-correction / auto-executed-tool-call (single happy-path call): this cell stresses coherence across a HIGH VOLUME of sequential tool calls in one turn.

sources.note: AssistantMessage(toolUse) -> assistant (stays working); ToolResults -> tool_result (closes the open call); ONLY a text-only AssistantMessage -> turn_done (the single ready settle); Prompt -> user_message (working). No waiting/permission path, so an intermediate tool result is never a settle/gate — the whole burst is one contiguous working span.
Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "kiro-cli"}; Source FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}; PID bound via DiscoverPID (cwd match) across the long turn.

Live 2.5.1 smoke: turn shape Prompt -> AM(text+toolUse) -> ToolResults -> AM(text+toolUse) -> ToolResults -> AM(text-only); text-only AM = turn_done; live append survives long turns; PID stays bound; JSONL has NO model/token/cost (sidecar holds credits/model); --trust-all-tools runs tools with no picker.

Sibling stress cell: structurally identical native many-tool-call single-turn burst (no permission gate). daemon=full / driver=ready, one contiguous working span, settles only at the turn-done signal. Calibration target for the kiro-cli recipe (kiro-cli --trust-all-tools matches codex's ungated tool execution); pins no model.

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep" — the recipe's three step types are all present and elicited; wait_turn counts text-only AssistantMessages (mirrors the parser's turn_done rule). driver=ready, no step to port.

preconditions: kiro-cli 2.5.1 on PATH and authenticated (subscription auth)
irrlichd is in --record mode with IRRLICHT_PERMISSION_MODE=grant-all
--trust-all-tools assumes persisted trust-all consent so no per-tool picker stalls the run
no other kiro-cli session shares the recipe's cwd
setup: The interactive driver launches `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` under tmux in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/; kiro mints its own session UUID and writes ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl lazily after the first prompt (discovered as the newest .jsonl after the launch marker).

No auto-mode step is needed: --trust-all-tools runs every tool with no permission gate (kiro-cli emits no AskUser/permission event), so the shell tool runs every command autonomously — this is exactly why kiro-cli captures the REAL many-tool-call single-turn burst, whereas the claudecode recipe for this scenario must fall back to 4 plain prompts.
The prompt is fully self-contained — it tells kiro-cli to CREATE its own files and then run many commands on them, so the per-run cwd starting empty does not shrink the burst.

wait_turn blocks until the turn's text-only AssistantMessage appears (mirroring the parser's turn_done rule), so the trailing sleep never races a still-running turn; the timeout is generous because this is a deliberately LONG turn.

script.text:
Do all of the following in THIS single turn, using your shell tool, running each as a SEPARATE command one at a time (do not combine them, do not write a script, do not ask me anything): 1) run `pwd`; 2) create three files with `printf 'line1
line2
line3
' > a.txt`, then `printf 'x
y
' > b.txt`, then `seq 1 20 > c.txt` (three separate commands); 3) run `ls -la`; 4) run `wc -l a.txt`, then `wc -l b.txt`, then `wc -l c.txt` (three separate commands); 5) run `cat a.txt`; 6) run `head -3 c.txt`; then finally summarize in one short paragraph how many lines each file had. Run every numbered command individually so I can see each one execute.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created (proc-<PID>) row in ready within 1000 ms of agent launch
events.jsonl contains the rollout session (kiro mints its own UUID) with a distinct session_id, born ready
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the rollout session_id (cwd match)
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to working on the rollout session_id when the prompt is sent (start of the agentic burst)
transcript.jsonl contains MANY AssistantMessage(toolUse) / ToolResults pairs in the single turn (at least 5: pwd, ls, and >=3 wc -l calls) — the high-volume burst
across the entire turn, NO state_transition to ready fires between successive tool-call/result pairs (one contiguous working span, no flicker mid-burst)
across the entire turn, NO state_transition to waiting fires (--trust-all-tools means no permission gate)
events.jsonl contains exactly one state_transition to ready on the rollout session_id once the final text-only AssistantMessage lands (turn end)
final session state is ready (not stuck working)
